item.title: Issue #2773 created
item.description:
ambiguous wording should be tightened up a tad

5.4.13 fn:hash, “Rules” section, para 2 says

The $algorithm argument determines the algorithm to be used to calculate a checksum, hash, or cyclic redundancy check. The effective value of the algorithm is determined by passing the value through fn:upper-case(fn:normalize-space()).

That second sentence would be easier to comprehend quickly if it read

  • “The effective value of this argument is determined by passing the value specified through fn:upper-case(fn:normalize-space()).”, or
  • “The effective value of this argument is determined by passing its specified value through fn:upper-case(fn:normalize-space()).”, or
  • “The effective value of $argument is …”, or
  • “The effective value of the $algorithm argument is …”, or, if you want to be really wordy and precise
  • “The effective value of this argument is determined by passing the xs:string specified as the second argument through fn:upper-case(fn:normalize-space()).”

(I realize I am dimmer than the average expected reader of the spec, but when I first read that sentence (albeit quickly, I admit) I really thought “Just uppercasing a string is a pretty useless hashing algorithm”. :​-​)
